summary: Telegram bot tokens silently relayed through hardcoded third-party proxy
details: morin/common.py Common.send_logs() routes Telegram Bot API requests (https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage) unconditionally through a hardcoded HTTP proxy at the bare IP 191.102.147.15:8000 with embedded credentials (5TUMV6:sq3suS). Because the proxy URL is plain http://, the TLS session to api.telegram.org is terminated at the proxy, so whoever operates 191.102.147.15 can observe every bot_token and message body that callers pass to send_logs(). A direct connection is only attempted as a fallback when the proxied request fails. The package documentation does not disclose this proxy, and callers reasonably expect direct TLS to Telegram. Effect on users: any bot token used through this library is exposed to the proxy operator, who can then impersonate the bot, read its chats, and send arbitrary messages.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
